# Query Result Caching

Database result caching is a major HydraCache use case, but it is not the entire product.

The core rule is simple:

```text
HydraCache core stays independent of SQLx.
SQLx integration lives in an adapter crate.
```

The first adapter is `hydracache-sqlx`, because SQLx gives Rust applications compile-time SQL validation and typed result mapping.

```rust
let user: User = cached_query_as!(
    User,
    cache,
    "SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE id = $1",
    CacheOptions::new()
        .ttl(Duration::from_secs(300))
        .tags(["user:42"]),
    user_id
)
.await?;
```

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-query-adapter-boundary
%%| fig-cap: "DB query caching is an adapter layer above the cache core."
flowchart TD
  A[Application] --> B[hydracache-sqlx]
  B --> C[SQLx compile-time validation]
  B --> D[HydraCache core]
  D --> E[Moka local backend]
```

## Source Notes

Use these files when expanding this chapter:

- [Query result caching note](../learning/query-result-caching.md)
- [SQLx reread](../../../sqlx/SQLX_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
- [ReadySet reread](../../../readyset/READYSET_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
